steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Set up the arc length formula
steps.content:
The length of an arc is proportional to its central angle. When a central angle spans $\theta$ degrees out of the full $360^{\circ}$, the arc length is:
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times C$$
where $C$ is the circumference of the circle.

steps.title: Substitute values and solve for circumference
steps.content:
We have an arc measuring $45^{\circ}$ with length $3$ inches. Substitute these values:
$$3 = \frac{45^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times C$$
Simplify the fraction $\frac{45^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} = \frac{1}{8}$:
$$3 = \frac{1}{8} \times C$$
Multiply both sides by $8$:
$$C = 24 \text{ inches}$$
That is the correct answer.
